commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b4ef2142da97fcbba421bea8495c921f000ae79a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR teaches session cleanup to repair stale generated `sessionFile` metadata to the canonical session transcript before `--fix-missing` pruning and adds integration tests for the repair cases.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +211. Total +283 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives concrete stale `sessionFile` plus canonical transcript examples, and current main still prunes based on one stored-path resolution; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the contributor must add real behavior proof and fix the visible repair reporting; no repair-only automation path is safe while the proof gate remains unsatisfied.

target.title: fix: preserve cron sessions with stale transcript metadata
target.body:
﻿Closes #50248

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running `sessions cleanup --fix-missing` could lose a fresh cron session row when its store entry still pointed at stale generated transcript metadata, even though the canonical transcript for the current session still existed and contained messages.

## Why This Change Was Made

Cleanup now repairs only the narrow stale-generated-base-transcript case before pruning: when a generated `sessionFile` belongs to another session id, the canonical `<sessionId>.jsonl` exists, and that canonical transcript has message records. The apply path rechecks the current store entry under the lifecycle mutation lock so concurrent session updates are not overwritten, and it deliberately does not rebind missing topic/custom transcript paths to the base transcript.

## User Impact

Fresh cron sessions with valid transcripts are preserved instead of being pruned by `--fix-missing`. Cleanup remains conservative for topic-specific or custom transcript paths, so unrelated missing-transcript entries still follow the existing prune behavior.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: stale generated `sessionFile` metadata is repaired before `--fix-missing` pruning, including the case where the stale transcript still exists; concurrent session-store writes are not clobbered; missing topic transcripts are not rebound to the base transcript.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `$env:OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS='1'; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts src/commands/sessions-cleanup.test.ts --reporter dot`
  - `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check --threads=1 src\config\sessions\cleanup-service.ts src\config\sessions\store.pruning.integration.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
- Observed result:
  - Vitest: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 43 passed (43)`.
  - oxfmt: `All matched files use the correct format.`
  - oxlint: exited 0 for the two touched files.
  - `git diff --check`: exited 0.
  - autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Not tested / proof gaps: no broad `pnpm check`, full typecheck, or live cron cleanup was run; this PR is validated with focused cleanup/session tests plus touched-file format/lint checks.
target.author: qingminglong
